mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 8.5% signals improving labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain capacity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through recessionary expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 2.5% combined with stable Fed funds rates signals central bank inaction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through real rate uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.2%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k growth signals weakening labor demand, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through corporate profit erosion expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through heightened corporate investment optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where both GDPC1 exceeds 3% and HOUST shows positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 6-month peak signals improving supply chain efficiency, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced inflationary pressures.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 6-month high.
